books.title: Stories From the History of Rome
books.author: Emily Beesly
books.rationale: This is a narrative introduction to the stories students will read later in Plutarch's Lives.

books.title: The Plutarch Project Primer: Publicola (Revised)
books.author: Anne White
books.rationale: Students will read about Publicola, one of the first consuls of the Roman Republic. The Plutarch Primer includes vocabulary, discussion questions, and other aids for students and parents/teachers, plus edited text for Plutarch's Life of Publicola. It is designed especially for those who are new to the study of Plutarch. Lesson plans will include additional guidance.

books.author: Anne White
books.rationale: Students will read about the lives of Julius Caesar, Agis and Cleomenes, and the Gracchi. Lesson plans will offer guidance as well.

books.title: Your Rugged Constitution
books.author: Bruce and Esther Findlay
books.rationale: This book guides students through the text of the Constitution with descriptions that are put in clear, easy-to-understand language, accompanied by commentary and lively drawings so all the ideas and concepts can be easily grasped.

books.title: Ourselves: Book 1
books.author: Charlotte Mason
books.rationale: Mason emphasized that children are persons; this is the volume in which she defines that term. What characteristics do we, as Image-Bearers, have in common? What kinds of pitfalls should we anticipate? These are some of the profound questions Mason deals with in this, her only volume written directly to students.

books.title: Who Runs This Country, Anyway?
books.author: Joanne Stanbridge
books.rationale: Who Runs This Country, Anyway is a fun, lighthearted, yet thorough introduction to the structure of Canada's government, its elected officials and the election process, the Canadian Constitution and its history, the important buildings and institutions found on Parliament Hill in Ottawa, and how bills become laws.

books.title: Reading PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Students will learn about important historical documents developed between 1800-1900. Following the founding of the country, the framework of the nation begins to take shape. The Missouri Compromise sets the stage for the Civil War as new states join the nation; the federal government experiences internal conflict as the Executive Branch disregards the Judicial Branch over the sovereignty of tribal nations; and the Reconstruction Amendments are added to the Constitution.  A "Reading PDF" will be included in the lesson plans that provides accessible analysis integrated with relevant excerpts of Supreme Court decisions, legislative acts, and other texts as appropriate.
books.sharedText:
American Gov./ Economics: Grade 8
Plutarch/Government: Grade 9 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 11 (U.S.)

books.title: Dollars and Sense: A Kid's Guide to Using - Not Losing - Money
books.author: Elaine Scott
books.rationale: In this introduction to Economics, students will learn about the history of money, the banking system, and personal finance.

books.author: Nathan Tidridge
books.rationale: Brimming with photographs and archival images, this book, written by a high school history teacher, is an accessible and extensive account of the role played by the Crown in Canada's history, Constitution, system of parliamentary government, other institutions, and the day-to-day lives of all Canadians.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.rationale: James Clear, one of the world's leading experts on habit formation, reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. This book brings Mason's thoughts on habits into the 21st century applying current research in this field.

books.title: The Prime Ministers: Canada's Leaders and the Nation They Shaped
books.author: J.D.M. Stewart
books.rationale: The Prime Ministers is a journey through Canada's political history through a chronological survey of its twenty-four Prime Ministers. Written specifically for high school students (and adults) to provide them with deeper knowledge of our nation's history, this book is less a collection of biographical sketches, and more a series of profiles focused on the defining events, policy decision, visions for the country, character strengths and weaknesses, missteps and triumphs, and leadership style of each Prime Minister.

books.title: Economics in One Lesson
books.author: Henry Hazlitt
books.rationale: This classic guide to the basics of economic theory defends capitalism and the free market from economic myths that persist to this day.

books.title: Citizenship Without Illusions: A Christian Guide to Political Engagement
books.author: David T. Koyzis
books.rationale: This book provides the reader with insightful ways to participate in the cultural and political landscape without setting aside one's spiritual convictions to do so. Koyzis addresses numerous topics such as voting, community engagement, and working with individuals with vastly different political ideologies. He grounds his approach in the reality that Christians are to love God with their heart, soul, mind, and strength, which results in loving their neighbor as themselves. He draws from decades of research, teaching, and student interactions, all from a global perspective, which makes this book a unique and timely resource.

books.title: The Life We're Looking For: Reclaiming Relationship in a Technological World
books.author: Andy Crouch
books.rationale: A deeply reflective primer on creating meaningful connections, rebuilding abundant communities, and living in a way that engages our full humanity in an age of unprecedented anxiety and loneliness.

books.title: Conscience: What It Is, How to Train It, and Loving Those Who Differ
books.author: Andrew Naselli & J.D. Crowley
books.rationale: This book is an exhortation to understand the basis of personal beliefs and relates how to extend grace when believers disagree.
